_README: Golden vectors for the playability metrics — the shared spec that any
must reproduce. PlayabilityMetrics_Tests.cs pins the C# implementation to
these values; a port in any other language is expected to pin to the same
file so the implementations cannot drift apart.
Every 'expected' value was computed BY HAND from the metric definitions
(see the 'arithmetic' field per entry) — do NOT regenerate them from an
implementation, that would make the spec circular and unable to catch a bug.
Sample convention: [x, y, z]. Planar travel is the XZ plane; Y is up.
pathLength   = sum of XZ distances between consecutive samples

vectors.name: straight_line
vectors.arithmetic: XZ steps 1+1+1 = pathLength 3. Net first->last (0,0)->(0,3) = 3. straightness 3/3 = 1.0. Y is flat so jumpDy = 0.

vectors.name: square_loop_returns_to_start
vectors.arithmetic: Four sides of length 2: pathLength 2+2+2+2 = 8. Net first->last = (0,0)->(0,0) = 0. straightness 0/8 = 0.0 (the circles bug). Y flat so jumpDy 0.

vectors.name: stuck_no_movement
vectors.arithmetic: All samples identical: pathLength 0. 0 < 1e-4 epsilon so straightness short-circuits to 0.0 (stuck is not straight). Y flat so jumpDy 0.

vectors.name: l_shaped_path
vectors.arithmetic: Leg 1 (0,0)->(3,0) = 3, leg 2 (3,0)->(3,4) = 4, pathLength 7. Net first->last (0,0)->(3,4) = sqrt(3^2+4^2) = sqrt(25) = 5. straightness 5/7 = 0.714285714285... Y flat so jumpDy 0.

vectors.name: clean_jump
vectors.arithmetic: XZ steps each 1 (Y ignored): pathLength 4. Net (0,0)->(0,4) = 4, straightness 4/4 = 1.0. jumpDy = max(y - y[0]=0) over arc 0,0.5,0.9,0.4,0 = 0.9.

vectors.name: dead_jump
vectors.arithmetic: XZ steps 1+1 = pathLength 2. Net (0,0)->(0,2) = 2, straightness 1.0. Y never rises above start 0 so jumpDy = 0 (the dead-jump bug).

vectors.name: jump_measured_from_start_index
vectors.arithmetic: All XZ identical so pathLength 0 -> straightness 0.0 (epsilon guard). jumpDy is relative to samples[1].y=0, peak 1.5 -> rise 1.5 (NOT relative to sample 0).

vectors.name: only_falling_no_rise
vectors.arithmetic: All XZ identical so pathLength 0 -> straightness 0.0. Y only decreases (5->3->1); rise never positive so jumpDy clamps to 0 (not -4).

vectors.name: single_sample
vectors.arithmetic: Fewer than 2 samples: pathLength 0 and straightness 0.0. jumpDy: only sample is the start sample, rise 0.

vectors.name: empty
vectors.arithmetic: No samples: all three metrics defined to return 0.
